pi_items.source: Calculated via Steinmetz solid volume formula (16/3 * r^3) for r=5.0
verifier.py geometric logic comments and mathematical bounds (differentiates Steinmetz ~666.67 from sphere ~523.6 and cube 1000)
Geometric bounding box calculation for intersecting orthogonal cylinders of diameter 10.0
verifier.py script configuration and reasonable tolerance for piecewise linear meshing approximations

privileged_info_summary: The task requires creating a Steinmetz solid from scratch by intersecting two orthogonal 10mm-diameter cylinders. The expected volume of 666.67 mm³ matches the mathematical formula for this shape (16/3 * r^3). The exact expected bounds of 10.0 mm along each axis are correct. A volume tolerance of 80.0 effectively isolates this specific shape from alternatives like a 10mm sphere (~523.6 mm³) or a 10mm cube (1000 mm³) while accommodating the piecewise linear mesh approximations used by SolveSpace.
